name: What kind of insurance should a general contractor have before working on my home?
headline: What kind of insurance should a general contractor have before working on my home?

mainEntity.name: What kind of insurance should a general contractor have before working on my home?
mainEntity.text: What kind of insurance should a general contractor have before working on my home?

acceptedAnswer.text: For general contractor work in Pacifica and nearby cities, liability and workers’ compensation insurance are very important. They protect both you and the contractor if something goes wrong. General liability insurance helps cover damage to your property caused by the contractor’s work. Workers’ compensation covers injuries to the contractor’s employees on your job. Without it, an injured worker might try to make a claim against you as the homeowner. Before work starts, ask the contractor to have their insurance agent send you a certificate of insurance listing you as the certificate holder. Check that the coverage limits look reasonable for the size of your project and that the dates will still be active during your job. Even if a contractor says they are “fully insured,” you should still see the paperwork. It’s not rude; it’s standard. Also make sure their name on the insurance matches the name on the contract and the contractor’s license. Be careful about hiring unlicensed or uninsured people to save money, especially for big projects or structural work. You might save at first, but you take on a lot more risk if there’s an accident, a fire, or damage that shows up later.
